evals.prompt: Create a README.md for my new Python CLI tool called 'dataflow' that processes CSV files
evals.expected_output: The agent creates a README with: project name and tagline, visual badges, quick start section (install + first usage), what is this section, core concepts, usage examples, contributing, license. Follows progressive disclosure structure.
assertions: The README has an H1 with project name and a one-sentence tagline
The README includes a Quick Start section with install and first usage
The README includes a 'What Is This?' or similar explanation section
All images (if any) have alt text

evals.prompt: Improve my existing README - it's just a wall of text with no structure
evals.expected_output: The agent restructures the README with: proper headings hierarchy, bullet points, code blocks with language tags, quick start section, and progressive disclosure.
assertions: The agent restructures with proper heading hierarchy (H1 → H2 → H3)
The agent breaks up walls of text into sections
The agent adds a quick start or installation section

evals.prompt: Does my README pass the 5-second test? Here's the current version: 'My Project is a versatile, powerful framework for building scalable applications.'
evals.expected_output: The agent evaluates against the 5-second rule: can a stranger understand the purpose in 5 seconds? Likely flags 'versatile, powerful framework' as vague and suggests a more specific tagline.
assertions: The agent applies the 5-second test criteria
The agent identifies vague or generic language
The agent suggests a more specific, benefit-focused tagline

evals.prompt: Add accessibility improvements to my README
evals.expected_output: The agent checks for: alt text on images, semantic heading order, descriptive link text, code blocks with language specified, and screen reader friendly emoji usage.
assertions: The agent checks for alt text on images
The agent ensures descriptive link text (not 'click here')

evals.prompt: Write a README for a Rust library with API documentation links
evals.expected_output: The agent creates a README following the template, including documentation section with links to docs.rs, API reference, and examples. Uses Rust-specific installation (cargo add).
assertions: The README includes Rust-specific install instructions (cargo add)
The README includes a Documentation section with doc links
The README follows the standard structure template
